title: Dividing decimals: thousandths
description: Practice dividing decimal numbers using "long division".
topic_path: Dividing a decimal by a decimal

hints:
$\begin{array}{rr}
&\orange{8}.\phantom{0}&\\
{35}&\overline{\Big)\blue{304}.5}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{280}\phantom{.0}\\
&\pink{24\phantom{.0}}
\end{array}$

$\blue{304} \div 35 = \orange{8}$ with a remainder of $\pink{24}$.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&8.\phantom{0}&\\
{35}&\overline{\Big)304.5}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{280}\phantom{.}{\downarrow}\\
&\blue{24\phantom{.}5}
\end{array}$

How many times does $35$ go into $\blue{245}$?

$\begin{array}{rr}
&8.\orange{7}&\\
{35}&\overline{\Big)304.5}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{280}\phantom{.0}\\
&\blue{24\phantom{.}5}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{24\phantom{.}5}\\
&\phantom{0.}0
\end{array}$

$\blue{245} \div 35 = \orange{7}$, with no remainder.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&8.7&\\
{35}&\overline{\Big)304.5}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{280}\phantom{.0}\\
&24\phantom{.}5\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{24\phantom{.}5}\\
&\phantom{0.}0
\end{array}$

There are no more digits to bring down, and the remainder is $0$. So the quotient is exactly $8.7$.
